title: Scanning Acoustic Microscopy (SAM)
sub_domain: High-frequency ultrasonic microscopy for buried interfaces
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in canonical NDT/NDE literature for this principle.

E.description: Scanning Acoustic Microscopy (SAM): scanning acoustic microscopy produces the measurement through a 4-node primitive DAG L.acoustic_lens -> S.scan.raster -> L.pulse_echo -> int.temporal, with time-integrated exposure and additive Gaussian thermal/electronic noise. Recovery is posed as a linear inverse problem that inverts the forward operator to estimate the scene-side 3D acoustic impedance. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~10; calibration-level mismatch (coupling_fluid_variation, surface_curvature, multiple_reflections) sets the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form imaging equation.
E.forward_model: y(t, x, y) = pulse conv reflectivity(x, y, z=c*t/2) + n; A/B/C-scan slicing
E.world_state_x: Defect / strain / composition map depending on principle

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 3D acoustic impedance is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by the declared priors. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~= 10); coupling_fluid_variation dominates the stability cliff; surface_curvature and the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Additive gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor, while mild Tikhonov or analytic inversion is sufficient at the nominal Omega point.

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [TOF-Gating, SAFT] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [SAM-CNN]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||x_hat - x||_2 <= C1 * sigma_noise / sqrt(N_measurements). Mismatch-induced bias adds + C2 * |coupling_fluid_variation| + C3 * ||x - x_s||_1 (best s-sparse / low-rank approximation residual). Iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the MLE; epsilon_fn captures the monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * log(...)) per iteration; learned variants: O(H W Z * F_theta_cost) per forward pass
